title: Metastelma pauciflorum
taxonomy: Metastelma pauciflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Metastelma, a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions. The plant is also known as the "few-flowered Metastelma" due to its sparse inflorescence. Metastelma pauciflorum is native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia. Further classification includes its rank as a species within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Metastelma pauciflorum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are used in folk medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant is also used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. In addition, Metastelma pauciflorum is used to treat gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.
